# BAS STUDIO — A STUDY IN MOTION

> BAS STUDIO is a studio that designs, builds, and deploys physical work — CNC sculpture, CAM fabrication, projection mapping, and realtime TouchDesigner media. A STUDY IN MOTION is its chronophotography relief installation: a single runner photographed in profile and broken across depth, milled into a stone-like panel and read by raking light, with a depth-mapped figure projected onto the relief in real time.

bas.run is a single-page site for the installation A STUDY IN MOTION by BAS STUDIO
(Conner Ward and Nick Eschen). It opens on an interactive torchlit WebGL relief and
walks through the pipeline that produced the gallery piece. Installed at Gray Area
Grand Theater, San Francisco — April 7, 2026.

## The work
- [A STUDY IN MOTION](https://bas.run/): a chronophotography relief installation — a runner's stride milled into relief and read by raking light, with realtime projection.

## The build pipeline
- Mold studies: depth/relief studies before milling.
- CNC fabrication: toolpath generated in Fusion 360, cut on a gallery-scale ShopBot in one pass (no tiling, no seams).
- Controller: a hardware MIDI controller patched into TouchDesigner as a tactile authoring surface and public gallery input.
- TouchDesigner: an operator network stacking dithered passes, depth-derived isophotes, and pixelation thresholds, keystoned live onto the milled relief.
- Lookdev: graded in DaVinci Resolve for gallery-throw contrast.
- Installation: the milled relief under raking light at Gray Area, with the source video in profile beside it.

## People & links
- [Conner Ward](https://connerkward.dev): creative technologist, founder of BAS STUDIO.
- [BAS STUDIO on Instagram](https://www.instagram.com/bas.runn/): studio account.
- [BAS on GitHub](https://github.com/connerkward/bas): source / build notes.
